name: The Golf Practice
about: The Golf Practice is a premier indoor golf training facility with locations in Highland Park and Lisle, Illinois. The Highland Park location spans 13,000 square feet and is equipped with 7 Trackman simulators, while the newer Lisle facility covers 15,000 square feet and features 8 Trackman simulators. Both locations offer extensive short game practice areas and a focus on player development through technology, coaching, and structured programs. The venues provide a professional environment for golfers to practice, receive instruction, and compete, with an emphasis on using data and technology to improve performance.
why_golfers_like_it: Cutting-edge Trackman technology provides detailed swing and ball data analysis
Focus on holistic player development with dedicated coaching for swing, mind, and body
State-of-the-art facilities offer extensive short game and putting practice areas
Structured youth programs cater to beginners and competitive junior golfers
Private event capabilities and club fitting services offer comprehensive golf solutions

food_and_drink.notes: No bar or alcohol service mentioned

simulator_hardware.notes: High-end launch monitor with dual radar technology providing precise ball and club data

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The Golf Practice provides all necessary equipment for practice sessions, though many golfers prefer to use their own clubs for familiarity.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: The website does not mention an on-site restaurant or bar, suggesting the facility may allow outside food and drinks for private events, but this should be confirmed when booking.

faq.question: How long does an 18-hole simulation take?
faq.answer: The website does not specify the exact time for a full 18-hole simulation, but typical simulator sessions range from 1-2 hours depending on the number of players and pace of play.

faq.question: Is this venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, The Golf Practice offers a comprehensive junior program with multiple age-specific introductory and competitive levels for children as young as 3 years old.
